transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Enumerate cheapest trees connecting Port, Ridge, Cove by considering candidate Steiner nodes and shortest connection structures.
Then account for coloring cost: because any tree is bipartite, at most 2 frequencies are ever needed when there is at least one interference edge among active villages; 1 frequency only if no active interference pair appears.
So optimize primarily edge cost, with +15 if zero active interference pairs, else +30.
Check direct and Steiner-mediated ways to reach Cove (likely via Cape) and Ridge (likely via Bay/Pine), while anchoring Port cheaply through Mill/Harbor/Rock/Grove.
Construct a few lowest-edge-cost feasible trees and compare whether they induce any interference edge.
Verify validity: chosen edges form a tree; frequencies assigned only to active villages; interference constraints satisfied.
If best candidate already clearly beats baseline and seems near-optimal, stop next turn; otherwise do one more subtask to prove optimality by lower-bound reasoning.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct and compare the lowest-cost feasible trees connecting Port, Ridge, and Cove, then assign minimal frequencies", "p_solve": 0.82, "time_budget_s": 180}

SUB_1: Trees are bipartite, so frequency cost is either 15 (if no active interference pair at all) or 30 (if at least one interference pair is active); 1 frequency is impossible here because at least one edge is needed.
SUB_1: So optimize edge cost first, but note we may pay +15 extra if any chosen active villages include an interference pair.
SUB_1: Cheapest way to include Cove is via Cape-Cove(2), since direct Cove links are 18 and 22.
SUB_1: Cheapest way to include Ridge is via Pine-Ridge(2) or Bay-Ridge(9)/Cliff-Ridge(13)/Grove-Ridge(14).
SUB_1: Need to connect Port to the rest cheaply: Port-Rock(2), Harbor-Port(3), Mill-Port(4), Grove-Port(8), Cape-Port(21), Cove-Port(22).
SUB_1: Best low-edge candidate found: Port-Rock(2), Rock-Harbor(2), Harbor-Grove(7), Grove-Bay(9), Bay-Ridge(9), Bay-Cape(17), Cape-Cove(2). Edge cost 48.
SUB_1: This is a valid tree on active vertices {Port,Rock,Harbor,Grove,Bay,Ridge,Cape,Cove}.
SUB_1: Active interference pairs present: Port/Rock, Harbor/Port, Harbor/Rock, Bay/Cape, Bay/Cove, Cape/Cove. So need 2 frequencies, total 48+30=78.
SUB_1: Better candidate: Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Ridge(9), Bay-Cape(17), Cape-Cove(2). Edge cost 40, active interference Bay/Cape and Bay/Cove and Cape/Cove, so total 70.
SUB_1: Better still: Port-Grove(8), Grove-Pine(16), Pine-Ridge(2), Port-Cove(22). Edge cost 48, interference Pine/Ridge only, total 78.
SUB_1: Key improvement is to avoid interference entirely and pay only one frequency.
SUB_1: Candidate with no interference pairs: Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Ridge(9), Mill-Cape(17), Cape-Cove(2). Edge cost 40.
SUB_1: Active vertices {Port,Mill,Bay,Ridge,Cape,Cove}; none of the listed interference pairs occur among these actives except Bay/Cape and Bay/Cove and Cape/Cove would occur, so this candidate actually has interference and is not no-conflict.
SUB_1: True no-conflict candidate: Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Cliff(20), Cliff-Ridge(13), Mill-Cape(17), Cape-Cove(2). Edge cost 64, no listed interference pair among {Port,Mill,Bay,Cliff,Ridge,Cape,Cove}; total 79.
SUB_1: Much better route to Ridge without Bay-Cape conflict: Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Pine(10), Pine-Ridge(2), Mill-Cape(17), Cape-Cove(2). Edge cost 43.
SUB_1: But this has interference Mill/Pine and Pine/Ridge, so total 73.
SUB_1: Strong candidate: Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Ridge(9), Port-Harbor(3), Harbor-Rock(2), Rock-? does not help reach Cove cheaply.
SUB_1: For Cove, direct Port-Cove(22) with Port-Mill-Bay-Ridge gives edge cost 43 and interference Bay/Cove only if Bay active, so total 73 (baseline structure variant).
SUB_1: Lower bound reasoning: connecting Cove needs at least 2 or 18 or 22; best is 2 via Cape but then Cape must connect onward by at least 17 to Mill/Bay or 21 to Port/23 to Dune, so Cove attachment costs at least 19.
SUB_1: Connecting Ridge to a Port-side component costs at least 11 via Bay-Pine-Ridge (9? actually Bay-Pine 10 + Pine-Ridge 2 from Bay side gives 12), or 13 via Cliff, or 9 via Bay-Ridge.
SUB_1: Connecting Port to network costs at least 0 if root, but to merge Cove-side and Ridge-side pieces likely minimum total edge near 40.
SUB_1: Found total 70 and several 73+, none below 70.
SUB_1: Best current solution appears the 5-edge tree with cost 70.
BEST_GUESS: {"edges":[["Port","Mill"],["Mill","Bay"],["Bay","Ridge"],["Bay","Cape"],["Cape","Cove"]],"frequencies":{"Port":1,"Mill":1,"Bay":1,"Ridge":1,"Cape":2,"Cove":1}}
UPDATED_PLAN_STATE: Best known feasible tree is Port-Mill, Mill-Bay, Bay-Ridge, Bay-Cape, Cape-Cove with total cost 70. One more subtask could try to prove optimality by tighter lower bounds or find an edge-cost-39 tree, but gains seem small.

DECISION: stop

SUB_2: I tightened the lower bound by splitting the tree into the unique branch point cases.
SUB_2: Since Cove is terminal, the cheapest way to include Cove is either Port-Cove(22), Mill-Cove(18), Bay-Cove(18), or Cape-Cove(2) plus one more edge out of Cape.
SUB_2: Thus cheapest attachment of Cove to the rest is 18 directly, or 2+17=19 via Cape-Mill, or 2+17=19 via Cape-Bay, or 2+21=23 via Cape-Port. So any feasible tree pays at least 18 for the Cove side.
SUB_2: For Ridge, cheapest attachment to the rest is Pine-Ridge(2)+one edge into Pine, or Bay-Ridge(9), or Cliff-Ridge(13), or Grove-Ridge(14), or Dune-Ridge(19).
SUB_2: To connect Ridge to Port-side cheaply, best options are Bay-Ridge with Bay ultimately linked to Port by Bay-Mill-Port costing 12 total from Bay to Port-side, or Pine-Ridge with Pine linked by Bay-Pine then Bay-Mill-Port costing 22 from Ridge to Port, etc.
SUB_2: I checked all plausible low-edge-cost trees under 40: if Cove uses direct 18 or 22, then Port-to-Ridge must be at most 21 or 17. Best Port-to-Ridge connection is Port-Mill-Bay-Ridge = 4+8+9 = 21, giving edge cost at least 39.
SUB_2: The only edge-cost-39 candidate from that bound is {Port-Mill, Mill-Bay, Bay-Ridge, Bay-Cove}. It is a valid tree.
SUB_2: But active interference pair Bay/Cove is present, so it needs 2 frequencies, total 39+30 = 69.
SUB_2: No edge-cost-38 tree exists by the above lower bound.
SUB_2: Therefore to beat 69, one would need edge cost <=38 with 2 frequencies, impossible, or edge cost <=53 with 1 frequency.
SUB_2: I checked one-frequency possibility: active set must avoid every listed interference pair. A cheap no-conflict route to Cove cannot use Bay-Cove or Cape-Cove with Cape plus Bay, and direct Cove-Port activates no conflict with Port/Cove.
SUB_2: Best no-conflict tree I found is Port-Cove(22) plus Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Ridge(9): edge cost 43, total 58.
SUB_2: Active vertices are {Port,Cove,Mill,Bay,Ridge}. None of the interference pairs occur among these active vertices except Bay/Cove, and yes both Bay and Cove are active, so this is not one-frequency.
SUB_2: Rechecking systematically, any tree using Bay-Ridge together with direct Bay-Cove has Bay/Cove conflict; any tree using Cape-Cove and Bay-linked Cape has Bay/Cape conflict; any tree using Pine-Ridge has Pine/Ridge conflict.
SUB_2: To avoid all conflicts while reaching Ridge and Cove, use Cliff-Ridge rather than Bay-Ridge/Pine-Ridge, and direct Port-Cove or Mill-Cove rather than Bay/Cove or Cape/Cove.
SUB_2: Cheapest such tree is Port-Mill(4), Mill-Bay(8), Bay-Cliff(20), Cliff-Ridge(13), Port-Cove(22) for edge cost 67, total 82.
SUB_2: So every no-conflict tree is far worse than the 2-frequency solutions.
SUB_2: Hence optimum is achieved by minimizing edge cost among feasible trees, and the proven minimum edge cost is 39.
SUB_2: Optimal total cost is 69.
SUB_2: One optimal tree is Port-Mill, Mill-Bay, Bay-Ridge, Bay-Cove with 2 frequencies.
